author: y4rr
content: Applied. ferrotorch-serialize/src/gguf.rs: 4 sites converted from Vec::with_capacity(attacker_u64)/HashMap::with_capacity(attacker_u64) to Vec::new()/HashMap::new() — bounds-checked read_bytes terminates loops naturally on truncated/adversarial input, no DoS surface. 9 new tests covering: 6-byte stub with u64::MAX, oversized-but-truncated buffer, mid-entry truncation, valid minimal GGUF still parses. Verification: cargo test PASS (153 unit + 1 ignored), cargo clippy -D warnings PASS, cargo fmt PASS, cargo build --workspace --all-targets PASS. No new error variants (uses existing InvalidArgument), no new deps, no public API change, no coordination needed. Subagent surfaced 3 additional findings for separate dispatch: (a) read_gguf_string u64-to-usize on 32-bit platforms (low severity), (b) alignment metadata unclamped, (c) n_dims unguarded (allows pathological 4B-iteration loops, though each iter is bounds-checked).
